disease.name: Hydronephrosis
disease.note: Dilation of renal pelvicalyceal system due to impaired urine drainage. Can be obstructive or non-obstructive. Severity graded by ultrasound.

severityAssessment.severityLabel: Mild (calyceal dilation only), Moderate (blunted calyces), Severe (thin cortex, parenchymal thinning). SFU grading 0-4.

subtypes.name: Obstructive Hydronephrosis
subtypes.management: Relieve obstruction: ureteric stent (double-J) or percutaneous nephrostomy if infected/severe. Definitive treatment based on cause (lithotripsy for stones, TURP for BPH, pyeloplasty for UPJO, ureterolysis for retroperitoneal fibrosis). Antibiotics if infected.

subtypes.name: Non-Obstructive Hydronephrosis
subtypes.management: Conservative. Serial ultrasound and renal function monitoring. Diuretic renogram to confirm non-obstructive. No intervention needed. Often resolves postpartum.

subtypes.name: Congenital Hydronephrosis (UPJO)
subtypes.management: Postnatal USS at 1-2 weeks. VCUG to rule out VUR. Diuretic renogram (MAG3) to assess obstruction. Mild-moderate: surveillance (ultrasound + renogram q3-6 months). Severe (>50mm AP diameter, split function <40%, recurrent symptoms): pyeloplasty (Anderson-Hynes).

imaging.name: Renal Ultrasound (KUB)
imaging.indication: AP diameter, calyceal dilation, cortical thickness, stone detection

imaging.name: Diuretic renogram (MAG3 or DTPA)
imaging.indication: obstructive vs non-obstructive, split renal function

imaging.name: CT urogram or NCCT KUB
imaging.indication: stone disease, level of obstruction, malignancy

management.protocolReference: EAU Guidelines on Urolithiasis / Pediatric Urology Guidelines

pathways.criteria: AP diameter <15mm, no calyceal dilation, normal function, no pain
pathways.treatment: Observation. Annual USS. Monitor renal function. No intervention.

pathways.criteria: symptomatic, AP 15-30mm, reduced split function >35%, pain
pathways.treatment: Relieve obstruction (stent or nephrostomy). Definitive treatment: pyeloplasty for UPJO, TURP for BPH, lithotripsy for stones, ureteric stent for retroperitoneal causes.

pathways.criteria: sepsis from obstruction, bilateral obstruction with AKI, split function <35%
pathways.treatment: Emergency decompression: percutaneous nephrostomy (preferred if infected/severe obstruction) or retrograde ureteric stenting. IV antibiotics. Resuscitation. Delayed definitive repair after inflammation resolves (6-12 weeks).

disease.emergencyPriority: high if bilateral/single kidney with AKI or infected
